def: A country on the west coast of South America.
A country in West Africa.
A small island city-state in South-East Asia.
The top part of the body, containing the brain and face.
The organ of the body used for seeing.
The part of the body where the arm joins the trunk.
The rear part of the body from the neck to the base of the spine.
The joint in the middle of the leg where it bends.
The organ inside the head that controls thought and the body.
The part of the face below the mouth.
The joint in the middle of the arm where it bends.
A hand with the fingers curled tightly into the palm.
The joint connecting the foot to the leg.
One of the curved bones that protect the chest.
A large organ in the body that cleans the blood.
A person's male child.
A person's female child.
The father of your mother or father.
The mother of your mother or father.
The brother of your mother or father, or your aunt's husband.
The sister of your mother or father, or your uncle's wife.
A female friend, or a woman one is in a relationship with.
The daughter of your brother or sister.
The father of your husband or wife.
The mother of your husband or wife.
Feeling sure that things will go well.
Annoyed because you cannot do or get what you want.
Feeling bad about something wrong you have done.
Unable to cope because there is too much to handle.
Unable to think clearly or understand something.
To feel irritated or angry about something.
To feel embarrassed or guilty about something.
To feel anxious about something.
Feeling or showing hope that good things will happen.
A mental illness marked by deep, lasting sadness.
Treatment aimed at healing an illness or mental problem.
A trained person who treats illness, often of the mind.
The state of being healthy, happy, and comfortable.
The sad feeling of being alone.
The practice of calming the mind through quiet focus.
A force pressing on something; also stress or strong demand.
The state of resting and letting go of stress.
A calm feeling of being happy with what you have.
The human mind and its emotions.
A helpful suggestion about what to do.
To rest and let go of tension.
To feel anxious about something.
Causing worry and mental strain.
Relating to feelings; showing strong emotion.
The state of being healthy and physically strong.
Keeping clean to stay healthy and prevent disease.
A medical treatment that protects you against a disease.
A doctor's judgement about what illness someone has.
A place where sick and injured people are treated by doctors.
A shop where you buy medicines and health products.
A place in the body where two bones meet and can move.
A place in the body where two bones meet and can move.
An illness caused by germs entering the body.
A controlled way of eating, often to lose weight or stay healthy.
To take air into the lungs and let it out again.
Lasting a long time or coming back again and again.
Reacting badly to a substance that is usually harmless.
A person who studies or works in science.
A test done to discover or prove something.
An idea suggested as a starting point, to be tested.
A set of ideas meant to explain how or why something happens.
A room equipped for scientific experiments and tests.
A careful study of something to understand its parts.
Facts and figures collected for study or reference.
The act of finding something for the first time.
The smallest unit of a chemical element.
A group of atoms bonded together, the smallest unit of a substance.
An instrument that makes tiny things look much larger.
All of space and everything that exists in it.
The science of substances and how they react and combine.
The science of matter, energy, and the forces of nature.
The science of living things.
A single living thing, such as a plant or animal.
The slow development of living things over many generations.
Relating to chemistry or to substances and their reactions.
Based on the methods and knowledge of science.
A plan for how much money can be spent.
Money that has been saved rather than spent.
A person who owns shares in a company.
A loan used to buy property, paid back over many years.
A bank account for keeping money and earning interest.
The percentage charged or paid for the use of money.
The amount of money currently in a bank account.
An arrangement for a company to take payments from your account.
A building where people live.
The main room in a home where people relax and sit together.
The money paid regularly to live in a home you do not own.
A machine that washes dishes.
A machine that washes dishes.
A hanging cloth that covers a window or stage.
A switch on the wall that turns a light on or off.
A box where letters are delivered or posted.
Having plenty of room; large inside.
Supplied with furniture.
Available to be rented out.
To live in a home that you rent rather than own.
A large container you fill with water to sit and wash in.
A metal device that heats a room.
A shelf in the kitchen for storing food or dishes.
The flat ledge at the bottom of a window.
A cloth spread over a table.
A comfortable outdoor seat for a garden or terrace.
A clock that hangs on a wall.
Words or drawings painted on public walls.
A street or area where only people on foot may go.
The large window at the front of a shop displaying goods.
Open to or shared by all people, not private.
Buses, trams, and trains that serve a local area.
The area around where you live and the people in it.
The basic laws and principles by which a country is governed.
Strong loyalty to one's own nation, often above others.
A harsh, authoritarian political system led by a dictator.
A change made to improve a system or law.
An agreement reached when each side gives up something.
A formal discussion where opposing views are argued.
A serious disagreement or struggle between sides.
Not taking either side in a disagreement or war.
A group of countries ruled by one powerful state.
The policy of one country controlling and settling others.
The highest social class, made up of noble families.
A system where the state controls almost every part of life.
The state of being free from control by others.
A country whose people mostly share one national identity.
A war between groups within the same country.
A line of rulers from the same family.
